commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=eb9a929110c7e6b4493a164ab8e63e0952b2bb9e)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds an optional `workDays` input to weekly pace calculation, threads `weeklyProgressWorkDays` through menu/store pace call sites, and adds focused `UsagePaceTests` for workday-aware weekly expectations.

Reproducibility: yes. for the compatibility concern from source: current main stores `weeklyProgressWorkDays` as a Display marker preference, and the PR reads the same value in pace calculation paths. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor proof plus maintainer/product judgment on stored setting semantics and historical pace precedence, so it is not a safe automated repair lane.

target.title: feat: factor weeklyProgressWorkDays into pace calculation
target.body:
## Summary

Threads the existing `weeklyProgressWorkDays` setting into the pace calculation so the expected usage curve distributes 100% across only the configured work days. Non-work days contribute zero expected usage, making the pace indicator accurate for users who only work on weekdays.

- Adds `workDays` parameter to `UsagePace.weekly()`
- Introduces `workdayAwareExpected()` which walks calendar days in the window and classifies each as work/non-work (Mon=1..Sun=7, workday if isoWeekday <= workDays)
- Passes `weeklyProgressWorkDays` from settings through all call sites
- Falls back to linear model when `workDays` is nil, 7, or window is not 10080 minutes

## Before / After

| Scenario (5-day user, Friday 18:00) | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| Expected usage | 82% (linear 7-day) | 95% (workday-aware) |
| 100% actual → pace verdict | **18% deficit** | **5% deficit** (on pace) |

## Test plan

- [x] `workday aware pace shows on track for five day user on friday` — verifies 7-day model reports deficit, 5-day model reports on-pace
- [x] `workday aware pace shows on track midweek` — Wed 18:00 with 60% used ≈ on pace for 5-day model
- [x] `workday aware pace falls back to linear when workDays is nil or 7`
- [x] `workday aware pace ignores non weekly windows` — session windows unaffected
- [x] All existing `UsagePaceTests` pass unchanged

Fixes #1356
Follow-up to #1096 (visual segmentation)
target.author: pstanton237
